overview: This week I progressed from a simple calculator tool to building a fully autonomous research agent in Python, implementing 7 tools across 3 domains and ~1,500 lines of code. I learned that tool chaining lets Claude reason through multi-step workflows without any IF/THEN logic from me, but autonomy comes with real costs — in API calls, context bloat, and unpredictable failures like hallucinated URLs. By Day 5, I was writing production-grade resilience infrastructure where 80% of the code existed just to handle failure modes.

keyPoints.title: Each tool use cycle requires a minimum of 2 API calls
keyPoints.description: A tool-enabled conversation costs 2–3× more than a basic exchange, and complex workflows can hit 4–6 API calls per user question — with full context sent every round trip.

keyPoints.title: Week ended with ~1,500 lines of Python across 7+ scripts
keyPoints.description: I implemented 7 tools across 3 domains and built 4 complete agents, plus a retry, logging, and stats framework I can reuse in every future agent.
keyPoints.sectionName: Key Numbers at the End of Week

keyPoints.title: 80% of Day 5 code was resilience, only 20% functionality
keyPoints.description: I built a robust tool execution framework with retry logic, exponential backoff, timeout handling, error categorization, rate limit detection, token tracking, and circuit breakers.

keyPoints.title: Research agent got only 4 successful fetches out of 20 attempts
keyPoints.description: Claude hallucinated plausible-sounding URLs that didn't exist, revealing that 'autonomous' doesn't mean 'omniscient' and that URL sourcing needs guardrails in production.

keyPoints.title: Cross-domain tool chaining produced autonomous workflows with zero orchestration logic
keyPoints.description: Combining ServiceNow mock tools, a web scraper, and a file utility let Claude autonomously research a problem, create a ticket, and save a resolution file — I wrote no workflow logic.
keyPoints.sectionName: Tool-Chaining Is Where Autonomous Workflows Begin

keyPoints.title: A single stray character silently removed a tool from Claude's toolkit
keyPoints.description: A malformed tool schema — one wrong character instead of a comma — caused Claude to tell users it lacked that capability entirely, costing me 20 minutes of debugging.
keyPoints.sectionName: Tool Descriptions Are Prompt Engineering, Not Documentation

keyPoints.title: Research agent context hit 10,000+ tokens per round trip by call five
keyPoints.description: Every API call includes full context plus all tool definitions (~1,500 tokens for a modest toolkit), meaning context management with agents isn't optional — it's load-bearing.
